goal: Add a side-effect-free application authority builder that records runtime patch consume application authorization from ready application preflight while keeping permit consume, patch application, server mutation, route mutation, runtime execution, packages, workflows, deployment, DB, providers, GitHub, and writes closed.
scope_out: Do not consume permits, apply runtime patches, mutate server files, mutate route files, run runtime execution, call providers, call DB, call GitHub, change package files, workflows, deployment files, or write files.
claims: Ready runtime patch consume application preflight can produce a side-effect-free application authorization artifact.
Application authority id, authorizer, and expiry refs are required.
Permit consume and runtime execution remain false.
assumptions: Actual permit consumption, patch application, and execution require later task locks.
dod: Ready, missing authority id, and blocked preflight cases are tested.
Focused test, typecheck, task-lock verify, and diff-check pass.
stop_conditions: Permit consumption, runtime patch application, server mutation, route mutation, runtime execution, external calls, DB, GitHub, package, workflow, deployment, or write changes become necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-125 preflighted runtime patch consume application without consuming the permit or executing runtime work.
